content.content:
1	# Claude Statusline
2	
3	A custom statusline for [Claude Code 2.x](https://claude.com/claude-code) that provides real-time usage tracking with an intelligent multi-layer progress visualization, helping you stay aware of context windows, costs, and session limits.
4	
5	## Features
6	
7	### Core Tracking Features
8	- **5-hour window tracker** - Real-time cost monitoring with burn-rate based projection and 3-layer visualization
9	- **Daily usage tracker** - 24-hour cycle aligned with weekly reset, shows % of weekly limit with end-of-day projection
10	- **Weekly usage tracker** - Multiple display modes: usage %, available %, or recommended daily usage
11	- **Monthly cost tracker** - Total spending from billing cycle start date (configurable)
12	- **Context window tracker** - 3-layer visualization showing cached vs fresh tokens, supports extended context (>168k)
13	- **Token burn rate** - Real-time tokens/min indicator based on billable tokens
14	- **Session timer** - Countdown to next 5-hour window reset with current/reset time display
15	- **Active sessions** - Concurrent Claude Code project counter
16	
17	### Advanced Features
18	- **Intelligent weekly calibration** - Compensates for untracked costs (deleted transcripts, extended context)
19	- **Multi-layer progression system** - Config-driven thresholds with auto-scaled visualization per layer
20	- **Weekly display modes** - Choose between usage %, available %, or recommended daily % to finish allocated budget
21	- **Daily projection integration** - Combines 5-hour window projection with daily tracking for accurate end-of-day estimates
22	- **Unified cache system** - Smart dependency tracking invalidates caches when config changes
23	- **Three-stage pipeline** - Clean architecture: Data Collection → Computation → Rendering
24	- **Conditional rendering** - Only computes enabled sections for optimal performance
25	- **Modular utilities** - Separate layer calculations, caching, and time tracking modules
26	
27	### Technical Highlights
28	- **Lightweight bash implementation** - Pure shell with minimal dependencies (jq, ccusage)
29	- **Privacy-first design** - Personal config and cache excluded from version control
30	- **Atomic cache writes** - Crash-safe tmp → mv pattern for all cache operations
31	- **Comprehensive config validation** - Startup validation prevents runtime errors
32	- **Tested on Max20 plan and macOS** - Compatible with Linux and WSL
33	
34	## Platform Support
35	
36	- **macOS** - ✅ Fully tested and supported
37	- **Linux** - ✅ Supported (requires bash, jq, npm)
38	- **Windows** - ⚠️ WSL or Git Bash only (not native CMD/PowerShell)
39	
40	## Example Output
41	
42	### Full Display (all sections enabled)
43	```
44	.claude | 45k/168k [████████░░] | $32/$140 [█████░░│░░] 23% | daily [██░│░░░░░░] 6/12% $21/$42 | total $324 | 5:45PM/10PM (3h 42m) | 928/min | ×2
45	```
46	
47	### Minimal Display (essential sections only)
48	```
49	.claude | $32/$140 [█████░░░░░] 23% | weekly 18% | 3h 42m
50	```
51	
52	**Section breakdown:**
53	- `.claude` - Current project directory (bright orange)
54	- `45k/168k [████████░░]` - Context: cached+fresh tokens with 3-layer progress bar
55	- `$32/$140 [█████░░│░░] 23%` - 5-hour window: cost with projection separator (│) and percentage
56	- `daily [██░│░░░░░░] 6/12% $21/$42` - Daily: actual/recommend % and cost (combined mode)
57	- `total $324` - Monthly total cost from billing cycle start
58	- `5:45PM/10PM (3h 42m)` - Current time / Reset time (countdown)
59	- `928/min` - Token burn rate (billable tokens per minute)
60	- `×2` - Active Claude Code sessions
61	
62	![Statusline Screenshot](./example/statusline.png)
63	*Screenshot showing the multi-layer color system in action*
64	
65	## Installation
66	
67	### Quick Install (Recommended)
68	
69	One command to install everything:
70	
71	```bash
72	curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hell0github/claude-statusline/main/install.sh | bash
73	```
74	
75	The installer will:
76	- ✅ Check dependencies (jq, ccusage)
77	- ✅ Copy files to ~/.claude/
78	- ✅ Prompt for your plan (pro/max5x/max20x)
79	- ✅ Ask permission before modifying settings.json
80	- ✅ Create backup of existing settings
81	- ✅ Guide you through setup
82	
83	### Manual Installation
84	
85	**Prerequisites:** [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code), `jq` (`brew install jq`), `ccusage` (`npm install -g ccusage`)
86	
87	```bash
88	# Clone repository to Projects directory
89	git clone https://github.com/hell0github/claude-statusline.git ~/cc-statusline
90	cd ~/cc-statusline
91	
92	# Run installer (recommended)
93	./install.sh
94	
95	# OR set up manually:
96	# 1. Create shim in ~/.claude/
97	cat > ~/.claude/statusline.sh << 'EOF'
98	#!/bin/bash
99	exec "$HOME/cc-statusline/src/statusline.sh" "$@"
100	EOF
101	chmod +x ~/.claude/statusline.sh
102	
103	# 2. Copy example config
104	cp config/config.example.json config/config.json
105	
106	# 3. Edit config (set your plan)
107	nano config/config.json
108	
109	# 4. Add to ~/.claude/settings.json:
110	{
111	  "statusLine": {
112	    "type": "command",
113	    "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh"
114	  }
115	}
116	
117	# Restart Claude Code
118	```
119	
120	## Configuration
121	
122	Edit `~/cc-statusline/config/config.json` to customize your statusline:
123	
124	### Essential Settings
125	- **`user.plan`** - Set to `"pro"`, `"max5x"`, or `"max20x"` (your subscription tier)
126	- **`limits.weekly`** - Weekly cost limits per plan (pro: $300, max5x: $500, max20x: $850)
127	  - Note: `pro` and `max5x` limits are estimated - only `max20x` has been verified
128	- **`limits.cost`** - 5-hour window cost limit (default: $140)
129	- **`limits.context`** - Context window limit in thousands (default: 168k)
130	
131	### Display Configuration
132	- **`display.bar_length`** - Progress bar length (default: 10 blocks)
133	- **`colors.*`** - Customize ANSI color codes for each element (green, orange, red, pink, cyan, etc.)
134	
135	### Multi-Layer Systems
136	- **`multi_layer.*`** - 5-hour window layers (3-layer system)
137	  - `layer1/2/3.threshold_multiplier` - Thresholds relative to cost_limit (default: 0.3/0.5/1.0)
138	  - `layer1/2/3.color` - Color names for each layer (default: green/orange/red)
139	
140	- **`daily_layer.*`** - Daily usage layers (2-layer system)
141	  - `layer1/2.threshold_multiplier` - Thresholds relative to base (default: 1.0/1.5)
142	  - `layer1/2.color` - Color names (default: green/bright_orange)
143	  - Base threshold is dynamic: weekly_limit/7 (static mode) or recommend value (dynamic mode)
144	
145	- **`context_layer.*`** - Context window layers (3-layer system)
146	  - `layer1/2/3.threshold_multiplier` - Thresholds relative to context_limit (default: 1.0/2.0/3.0)
147	  - `layer1/2/3.color` - Color names (default: dim_pink/dim_orange/dim_red)
148	  - Allows tracking beyond nominal 168k limit
149	
150	### Section Toggles
151	- **`sections.show_directory`** - Project name display (default: true)
152	- **`sections.show_context`** - Context window tracker (default: true)
153	- **`sections.show_five_hour_window`** - 5-hour cost window (default: true)
154	- **`sections.show_daily`** - Daily usage tracker (default: true, requires `official_reset_date`)
155	- **`sections.show_weekly`** - Weekly usage display (default: true)
156	- **`sections.show_monthly`** - Monthly total cost (default: false, requires `payment_cycle_start_date`)
157	- **`sections.show_timer`** - Reset countdown (default: true)
158	- **`sections.show_token_rate`** - Token burn rate (default: true)
159	- **`sections.show_sessions`** - Active session counter (default: true)
160	
161	### Weekly Display Modes
162	- **`sections.weekly_display_mode`** - How to display weekly section:
163	  - `"usage"` - Shows weekly usage % (default, label: "weekly")
164	  - `"avail"` - Shows available % remaining (label: "avail")
165	  - `"recommend"` - Shows recommended daily % to finish allocated budget (label: "recom")
166	    - Formula: `(weekly_limit - usage_from_weekly_start_to_daily_cycle_start) / cycles_left`
167	    - Stable throughout each daily cycle (e.g., 3pm→3pm), updates only at daily reset
168	    - Uses raw weekly cost (excludes baseline) for full limit availability
169	    - Combines with daily tracker to show: `daily [bar] actual/recommend% $actual/$recommend`
170	    - Example: Day 1 at 3pm: $850 / 7 days = $121/day (14%)
171	
172	### Tracking Configuration
173	- **`tracking.weekly_scheme`** - Weekly calculation method:
174	  - `"ccusage"` - ISO weeks Monday-Sunday (default)
175	  - `"ccusage_r"` - Official Anthropic reset schedule (requires `official_reset_date`)
176	
177	- **`tracking.official_reset_date`** - ISO 8601 timestamp for Anthropic weekly reset
178	  - Format: `"2025-10-08T15:00:00-07:00"` (from console "Resets Oct 8, 3pm" in America/Vancouver)
179	  - Required for: daily tracking, ccusage_r scheme, recommend mode
180	
181	- **`tracking.payment_cycle_start_date`** - ISO 8601 timestamp for billing cycle start
182	  - Format: `"2025-10-01T15:00:00-07:00"` (date is cycle start, time copied from official_reset_date)
183	  - Required for: monthly cost tracking (show_monthly)
184	
185	- **`tracking.weekly_baseline_percent`** - Compensation for untracked costs (default: 0)
186	  - Adds fixed % to weekly usage to account for deleted transcripts
187	  - Use calibration tool to calculate: `tools/calibrate_weekly_usage.sh <official_%>`
188	
189	- **`tracking.cache_duration_seconds`** - Cache TTL in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)
190	  - Controls how often ccusage_r queries run
191	  - Lower values = more responsive but more API calls
192	
193	See `config/config.example.json` for all available options with detailed comments.
194	
195	## Handling Untracked Costs (Deleted Transcripts)
196	
197	**Problem**: When you use Claude Code's `clear` or `compact` commands to delete transcripts, the usage data is permanently removed from ccusage tracking (which reads from transcript files). This creates a discrepancy:
198	- **Anthropic Console**: Still shows full usage (server-side records)
199	- **Statusline**: Shows lower percentage (missing deleted transcript costs)
200	
201	Additionally, extended context usage (e.g., Sonnet 4 [1m]) may have pricing differences that create tracking gaps.
202	
203	**Solution**: Use the built-in calibration tool to automatically calculate and apply the baseline offset.
204	
205	### Automated Calibration (Recommended)
206	
207	**Option 1: Slash Command** (Available in any Claude Code session)
208	```bash
209	# From any project
210	/calibrate_weekly_usage_baseline 18.5
211	```
212	
213	**Option 2: Direct Script**
214	```bash
215	# From statusline project directory
216	cd ~/cc-statusline
217	tools/calibrate_weekly_usage.sh 18.5
218	```
219	
220	**What it does:**
221	1. Reads your current tracked usage from ccusage_r
222	2. Calculates the gap: `official_% - tracked_%`
223	3. Updates `tracking.weekly_baseline_percent` in config
224	4. Clears cache to force refresh
225	
226	**When to calibrate:**
227	- After weekly reset (to zero out baseline if needed)
228	- When you notice drift between statusline and console
229	- After significant transcript cleanup operations
230	- Weekly as a maintenance routine
231	
232	**Prerequisites:**
233	- `tracking.weekly_scheme` must be `"ccusage_r"`
234	- `tracking.official_reset_date` must be configured
235	
236	### Manual Calibration (Alternative)
237	
238	If you prefer manual configuration:
239	
240	1. **Check Anthropic console** for current weekly usage %
241	2. **Check statusline** for current weekly usage %
242	3. **Calculate gap**: `console_% - statusline_%`
243	4. **Update config**:
244	   ```json
245	   {
246	     "tracking": {
247	       "weekly_baseline_percent": 5
248	     }
249	   }
250	   ```
251	
252	**Example:**
253	- Console shows: **18.5%**
254	- Statusline shows: **12.3%**
255	- Gap: `18.5% - 12.3% = 6.2%`
256	- Set: `"weekly_baseline_percent": 6.2`
257	- Result: Statusline now shows **18.5%** ✓
258	
259	**Note**: The baseline applies to **all tracking schemes** (both `ccusage` and `ccusage_r`). If you don't delete transcripts, keep this at `0` (default).
260	
261	## Daily and Weekly Usage Tracking Calibration
262	
263	**Note**: By default, weekly tracking uses ISO weeks (Monday-Sunday) via ccusage. This may show different percentages than the Anthropic console, which uses custom reset cycles (e.g., Wednesday 3pm → Wednesday 3pm).
264	
265	### Configure Official Reset Schedule (Enables Daily Tracker)
266	
267	Setting the official reset date **enables two features**:
268	1. **Daily usage tracking** - 24-hour cycle tracker with end-of-day projection
269	2. **Weekly tracking calibration** - Match Anthropic console percentage exactly (when using `ccusage_r` scheme)
270	
271	**Setup steps:**
272	
273	1. **Find your reset date** at [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com):
274	   - Go to Usage tab
275	   - Look for "Resets [date/time]" text (e.g., "Resets Oct 8, 3pm")
276	
277	2. **Update your config** (`~/cc-statusline/config/config.json`):
278	```json
279	{
280	  "tracking": {
281	    "weekly_scheme": "ccusage_r",
282	    "official_reset_date": "2025-10-08T15:00:00-07:00"
283	  }
284	}
285	```
286	
287	3. **Format guide**:
288	   - `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS±HH:MM`
289	   - Example: "Oct 8, 3pm Vancouver" → `2025-10-08T15:00:00-07:00` (PDT = UTC-7)
290	   - You only need to update this once; it auto-calculates future periods
291	
292	**Results**:
293	- Daily tracker will appear in your statusline showing today's usage as % of weekly limit
294	- Weekly percentage will match Anthropic console (if using `ccusage_r` scheme)
295	
296	**Note**: Daily tracking works with either `ccusage` or `ccusage_r` weekly schemes - only `official_reset_date` is required.
297	
298	## Troubleshooting
299	
300	| Issue | Solution |
301	|-------|----------|
302	| Statusline doesn't appear | `chmod +x ~/.claude/statusline.sh` and restart Claude Code |
303	| "jq: command not found" | `brew install jq` (macOS) or `sudo apt-get install jq` (Linux) |
304	| "Window tracking unavailable" | `npm install -g ccusage` |
305	| Wrong usage data | Update `user.plan` in config, verify with `ccusage blocks --active` |
306	| Colors not working | Check terminal 256-color support |
307	
308	## Architecture
309	
310	### Shim Pattern
311	This plugin uses a **shim architecture** for clean separation and stability:
312	
313	```
314	Claude Code → ~/.claude/statusline.sh (2-line shim)
315	                      ↓
316	              ~/cc-statusline/src/statusline.sh (implementation)
317	```
318	
319	**Benefits:**
320	- Stable interface: Claude Code always calls the same shim path
321	- Flexible implementation: Can reorganize code without reconfiguring Claude
322	- Easy updates: Git pull updates implementation without touching shim
323	
324	### Three-Stage Pipeline
325	The statusline follows a clean data flow architecture:
326	
327	```
328	┌─────────────────────┐
329	│  STAGE 1: COLLECT   │  Parse input, call ccusage, read transcripts
330	│                     │  • Conditional: only for enabled sections
331	│                     │  • All external process calls happen here
332	└──────────┬──────────┘
333	           ↓
334	┌─────────────────────┐
335	│  STAGE 2: COMPUTE   │  Calculate percentages, layers, projections
336	│                     │  • Apply layer calculation functions
337	│                     │  • Pure computation, no external calls
338	└──────────┬──────────┘
339	           ↓
340	┌─────────────────────┐
341	│  STAGE 3: RENDER    │  Build progress bars, apply colors, format
342	│                     │  • Assemble sections with separators
343	│                     │  • Output final statusline string
344	└─────────────────────┘
345	```
346	
347	**Benefits:**
348	- Clear separation of concerns
349	- Easier to test individual stages
350	- Obvious data dependencies
351	- Conditional evaluation for performance
352	
353	### Modular Utilities
354	- **statusline-utils.sh** - Time period calculations (ISO↔Unix, daily/weekly/monthly boundaries)
355	- **statusline-layers.sh** - Generic 2-layer and 3-layer visual scale calculations
356	- **statusline-cache.sh** - Unified cache management with dependency tracking
357	
358	### Caching Strategy
359	Smart caching system with multiple invalidation triggers:
360	- **Period change** - Daily/weekly/monthly boundary crossed
361	- **Time-based** - Cache older than configured duration (default: 5 min)
362	- **Dependency change** - Config values affecting calculations changed (weekly_limit, baseline)
363	- **Data corruption** - Cached value fails validation
364	
365	All cache writes are atomic (tmp → mv) for crash safety.
366	
367	## File Structure
368	
369	```
370	~/cc-statusline/          # Installation directory
371	├── src/                           # Source code
372	│   ├── statusline.sh             # Main implementation (3-stage pipeline)
373	│   ├── statusline-utils.sh       # Time tracking utilities (daily/weekly/monthly)
374	│   ├── statusline-layers.sh      # Generic layer calculation functions
375	│   └── statusline-cache.sh       # Unified cache management with dependency tracking
376	├── config/                        # Configuration
377	│   ├── config.json               # Your settings (gitignored)
378	│   └── config.example.json       # Template with defaults
379	├── data/                          # Runtime cache (gitignored)
380	│   ├── .daily_cache              # Daily cost cache (period-aware)
381	│   ├── .official_weekly_cache    # Weekly cost cache (ccusage_r scheme)
382	│   ├── .monthly_cache            # Monthly cost cache (billing cycle)
383	│   ├── .weekly_recommend_cache   # Recommend value cache (cycle-aware)
384	│   ├── .cache_deps               # Config dependency tracking (invalidation)
385	│   └── statusline-data.json      # Legacy cache (deprecated)
386	├── tools/                         # Utilities
387	│   └── calibrate_weekly_usage.sh # Weekly usage calibration tool
388	├── example/                       # Example screenshots
389	│   └── statusline.png            # Visual reference
390	├── install.sh                     # Automated installer
391	├── README.md                      # This file
392	├── CLAUDE.md                      # Development guide (architecture, conventions)
393	├── LICENSE                        # MIT License
394	└── .gitignore
395	
396	~/.claude/
397	├── statusline.sh                 # 2-line shim (delegates to src/statusline.sh)
398	└── commands/                      # Slash commands (optional)
399	    └── calibrate_weekly_usage_baseline.md  # Calibration slash command
400	```
401	
402	**Key components:**
403	- **statusline.sh** - Entry point, config validation, 3-stage rendering pipeline
404	- **statusline-utils.sh** - Period calculations (daily/weekly/monthly), ccusage_r support
405	- **statusline-layers.sh** - Reusable 2-layer and 3-layer metric calculations
406	- **statusline-cache.sh** - Centralized cache validation with config dependency tracking
407	- **Caches** - Atomic writes (tmp → mv), period-aware validation, dependency invalidation
408	- **Shim** - Stable interface in ~/.claude/, implementation in ~/cc-statusline/
409	
410	## License
411	
412	MIT License - See [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
413	
414	Free to use, modify, and distribute. No warranty provided.
415	
416	## For Developers
417	
418	### Quick Start
419	
420	Contributing or modifying the plugin? See [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) for comprehensive development documentation:
421	
422	- **Architecture** - Shim pattern, 3-stage pipeline, modular utilities
423	- **Development principles** - Open-closed principle, config-driven design
424	- **Testing** - Manual testing commands, function testing procedures
425	- **Git workflow** - Commit conventions, branching strategy
426	- **Path conventions** - Relative path standards, script directory references
427	- **Recent updates** - Version history and feature changelog
428	
429	### Key Principles
430	
431	**Open-Closed Principle:**
432	- All config values loaded from JSON - NEVER hardcode in scripts or comments
433	- Use threshold multipliers, not absolute values
434	- Single source of truth for all configuration
435	
436	**Examples:**
437	```bash
438	# ❌ Bad: Hardcoded threshold
439	LAYER1_THRESHOLD=14.29
440	
441	# ✅ Good: Config-driven calculation
442	LAYER1_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {print $BASE * $LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
443	```
444	
445	**Testing workflow:**
446	```bash
447	# Test main script
448	echo '{"workspace":{"current_dir":"~"},"transcript_path":""}' | src/statusline.sh
449	
450	# Test utility functions
451	source src/statusline-utils.sh
452	get_daily_cost "2025-10-08T15:00:00-07:00"
453	
454	# Test calibration tool
455	tools/calibrate_weekly_usage.sh 18.5
456	```
457	
458	**File organization:**
459	- `src/statusline.sh` - Main script (keep clean, delegate to utilities)
460	- `src/statusline-*.sh` - Utility modules (self-contained, reusable functions)
461	- `tools/` - Standalone tools (calibration, maintenance)
462	- `config/` - User config (gitignored) + example template
463	
464	### Contributing
465	
466	1. Fork the repository
467	2. Create a feature branch
468	3. Follow development principles in CLAUDE.md
469	4. Test thoroughly with your plan (pro/max5x/max20x)
470	5. Submit pull request with clear description
471	
472	**Development repository:** `git@github.com:hell0github/claude-statusline-dev.git`
473	
474	## Acknowledgments
475	
476	- Plugin for [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
477	- Uses [ccusage](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ccusage) for usage tracking
478	- Inspired by [Claude-Code-Usage-Monitor](https://github.com/Maciek-roboblog/Claude-Code-Usage-Monitor)
479	- Thanks to the community for better usage awareness in AI-assisted coding
480	
481	---
482	
483	**Note:** This is an unofficial third-party tool and is not affiliated with or endorsed by Anthropic.
484

content.text:
Here is a complete inventory of everything cc-statusline supports, drawn from your live `config/config.json` and the README.

---

## Sections (all in `sections.*`)

| Key | What it shows | Your current state |
|---|---|---|
| `show_directory` | Current project name (bold bright orange) | ON |
| `show_context` | Context window: cached+fresh tokens, 3-layer progress bar | ON |
| `show_session` | 5-hour cost window: `$X/$Y [bar] %` with burn-rate projection separator | ON |
| `show_daily` | Daily cycle tracker: actual/recommend % and cost | ON |
| `show_weekly` | Weekly usage (mode-dependent, see below) | ON |
| `show_monthly` | Total cost from billing cycle start date | ON |
| `show_timer` | Countdown to next 5-hour window reset with current/reset time | OFF |
| `show_token_rate` | Real-time token burn rate (tokens/min) | OFF |
| `show_sessions` | Active concurrent Claude Code session count | ON |

---

## Weekly Display Mode (`sections.weekly_display_mode`)

Three options, your current setting is `"recommend"`:

| Value | Label shown | What it displays |
|---|---|---|
| `"usage"` | `weekly` | Weekly usage % consumed |
| `"avail"` | `avail` | Remaining % available |
| `"recommend"` | `recom` | Recommended daily % to finish budget on schedule. When active, merges with daily section to show `daily [bar] actual/recommend% $actual/$recommend` |

---

## Limits (`limits.*`)

| Key | Current value | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `limits.cost` | `50` | 5-hour window cost cap (dollars) |
| `limits.context` | `1000` | Context window limit (thousands of tokens, so 1,000k) |
| `limits.token` | `220000` | Token threshold for burn-rate tracking |
| `limits.weekly.max5x` | `500` | Weekly dollar limit for your plan |

---

## Multi-Layer Progress Systems

Each system has config-driven thresholds (as multipliers of the base limit) and a color per layer.

**5-hour session (`multi_layer.*`)** — 3 layers:
- Layer 1: 0–0.3x cost limit → green
- Layer 2: 0.3–0.5x → orange
- Layer 3: 0.5–1.0x → red

**Daily tracker (`daily_layer.*`)** — 2 layers:
- Layer 1: 0–1.0x base (weekly/7 or recommend) → dim_green
- Layer 2: 1.0–1.5x base → dim_orange

**Context window (`context_layer.*`)** — 3 layers:
- Layer 1: 0–1.0x context limit → purple
- Layer 2: 1.0–2.0x → orange
- Layer 3: 2.0–3.0x → red

---

## Display Options (`display.*`)

| Key | Current value | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `bar_length` | `10` | Number of blocks in each progress bar |
| `transcript_tail_lines` | `200` | Lines read from transcript for burn-rate |
| `session_activity_threshold_minutes` | `5` | Minutes of inactivity before session is considered idle |
| `show_labels` | `true` | Show text labels like "daily", "recom", "total" (false = concise) |

---

## Tracking Options (`tracking.*`)

| Key | Current value | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `weekly_scheme` | `"ccusage"` | ISO weeks (Mon–Sun). Change to `"ccusage_r"` to match Anthropic console schedule |
| `official_reset_date` | `` (empty) | Required for daily tracker and `ccusage_r` scheme. Set from your console's "Resets" date |
| `payment_cycle_start_date` | `2025-05-01T00:00:00+00:00` | Billing cycle start for monthly cost tracking |
| `weekly_baseline_percent` | `0` | Offset to compensate for costs from deleted transcripts |
| `cache_duration_seconds` | `300` | How long ccusage_r results are cached (5 min) |

---

## Key Notes on Your Current Config

- `show_timer` and `show_token_rate` are both **OFF** — enable them in `sections` if you want the reset countdown or tokens/min.
- `official_reset_date` is **empty** — this means the daily tracker (`show_daily` is ON) may not work correctly. It requires that field to know your 24-hour cycle boundaries.
- `weekly_scheme` is `"ccusage"` (ISO weeks), so weekly % may differ from what the Anthropic console shows. To match the console exactly, set `official_reset_date` and switch to `"ccusage_r"`.
- `show_monthly` is ON and `payment_cycle_start_date` is set, so monthly total should be active.

---

For future changes to the status line setup (toggles, new sections, command changes), use the **statusline-setup** agent — you can ask Claude directly to make further adjustments at any time.
